name: Jabir ibn Abdullah
title: The First Pilgrim
shortDescription: The old and blind companion of the Prophet (PBUH) who, as the first pilgrim, reached Imam Hussain's (AS) tomb on the day of Arbaeen.
fullStory:
Jabir ibn Abdullah al-Ansari was a great and loyal companion of the Holy Prophet (PBUH) who was present alongside him in many battles. He was a true lover of the Ahl al-Bayt (AS) and narrated many Hadiths regarding their virtues.

At the time of the Ashura event, Jabir was a blind old man and could not be present in Karbala. However, upon hearing the news of Imam Hussain's (AS) martyrdom, despite his old age and blindness, he decided to visit his tomb.

Accompanied by Atiyya al-Awfi, he reached Karbala on the 20th of Safar (Arbaeen). Jabir performed Ghusl (ritual bath) in the Euphrates, perfumed himself, and guided by Atiyya, placed his trembling hands on the soil of the Master of Martyrs' grave.

He cried out 'Ya Hussain' three times and fainted. After regaining consciousness, he recited a Ziyarat and addressed the martyrs saying: 'By God, we are partners in what you entered into.' Jabir established the tradition of the Arbaeen pilgrimage in history.
quote: (His cry at the Imam's grave:) Does a friend not answer his friend?... How can you answer when your blood has been shed and your head separated from your body?
